items.source: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases
items.title: FSTB and HKMA to further unlock potential of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed income market

sources.name: Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau — Press releases

items.summary: The Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced further measures to expand the use of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed-income market.
items.so_what: Signals a continuing official push to make tokenised debt mainstream in Hong Kong — expect follow-on guidance touching settlement, custody and record-keeping controls.

items.title: The European Banking Authority consults on a draft methodology for setting fines under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA)
items.summary: The European Banking Authority is consulting on a draft methodology for setting fines on issuers it supervises under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation.
items.so_what: A concrete preview of how penalties under the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation will be sized — a useful benchmark when gauging the EU enforcement exposure of stablecoin and token issuers.

items.title: The EBA updates validation rules for supervisory reporting
items.summary: The European Banking Authority published updated validation rules for supervisory reporting, flagging corrections reporting institutions should apply.
items.so_what: Routine, but worth passing to regulatory-reporting teams handling EU entities, including MiCA-related returns.
